programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Dr Sloan discovers the identity of the laughing-gas killer and sets a trap for him, while Jack mounts a sting operation to recover Amanda's stolen money. Detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan's insufferable sister discovers a dead estate estate agent in her new house - and the medical sleuth's investigation throws up no shortage of suspects. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A successful boxer is found murdered shortly after a bout, so Jack steps into the ring to catch the culprit. Detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team travels to Ireland to mark one year since Megan's death, and Walter discovers a disaster at a nearby lake has created a poisonous cloud that could wipe out a village

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Paige's estranged mother enlists the team's help when she accidentally uncovers a forgotten nuclear reactor, which is on the verge of exploding. Drama inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An enigmatic killer shoots dead a businessman spending Thanksgiving at a hotel in the city, then takes his glasses, jewellery and ID to Washington Dulles airport, which has been brought to a stand-still by a massive snowstorm. As the murderer's actions take effect, a terror alert is issued, leaving all northern US airports in a state of panic, and leaving it up to DiNozzo, Bishop and her husband - all of whom are trapped at Dulles by the weather - to bring the armed villain to justice. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The bodies of five people, all of whom are identified as being from the US Navy, are found on the floor of a diner, and all are connected to a suspicious tax firm that specialises in helping people who are in arrears. When further killings are linked to the business, the team realises the crime seem to be copycats of previous cases. Meanwhile, two of Gibbs' ex-wives appear at the location with something important to discuss with the agent. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: American social worker Sarah Goode is taken hostage by militants in northern Syria, and thanks to the efforts of the Navy Seals, is eventually returned home unharmed. Evidence from the incident suggests US citizen Kyle Nasry was connected to the kidnapping, but when the agents trace Nasry to a rally held by a controversial imam, they are surprised to find Sarah among the attendees. Meanwhile, DiNozzo finally takes his relationship with Zoe Keates public - surprising none of his colleagues. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his colleagues investigate when Eric Kutzler, the first openly gay Navy lieutenant tipped to receive the Medal of Honour, is murdered. While the agents initially suspect Kutzler's death was the result of a hate crime, the fact that his police officer husband waited 20 minutes to report the crime suggests he had something to hide. Meanwhile, the team throws a baby shower for Palmer as his wife's due date approaches. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. Hetty and the NCIS team continue trying to find the elusive black book, but face competition from foreign security agencies keen to discover the secrets it contains - and Kensi is kidnapped as the search narrows

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The agents investigate the deaths of two men killed in a shoot-out, but have to rely on a former naval intelligence officer suffering from post-traumatic stress disorder to help them close the case

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and the team are called in to look for the body of a murdered Muslim man that has gone missing from the morgue. As they try to establish a link between a top-secret residue found on the corpse and the victim's killers, their investigations uncover an experimental Navy tracking system and a plot to sell nuclear material to terrorists planning to make a bomb. Starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Following a string of humiliating set-ups by teenagers, Gina clamps down on hoax calls and assigns Will to screen all incoming messages, but the plaintive voice of a youngster claiming he and his brother have been kidnapped by their father convinces Nikki it's the real thing. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sally and Ben are shocked to find a 12-year-old at the wheel of a stolen vehicle belonging to known violent offender Dylan Morrison. Paul Copley guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A homeless man is hit on the head by 'bricks' that turn out to be bags of cocaine jettisoned by Ben's protege Liam Harvey. When the lad is later found badly beaten, the kindly copper is incensed, assuming his father Bob is responsible. Under provocation during the interrogation, he puts his career on the line by head-butting the suspect and it's left to Callum to save Ben's skin when DPS officers arrive to investigate the resulting complaint

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Period medical drama chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. Jemma Redgrave stars

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Wealthy widow Alice Costigan invites Robert to stay at her country house for a spot of pheasant shooting, filling him with high hopes of romance. However, he's not the only one vying for her affections, and when her nephew falls ill she snubs him by turning to his rival for help. Victorian medical drama, starring Jemma Redgrave, David Calder, Maureen Beattie and Andrew Connolly

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Marsham learns his wife has breast cancer and tries to make amends for neglecting her, shelving a planned move to Scotland and begging Robert and Eleanor to perform a life-saving operation. Medical drama, starring Jemma Redgrave and David Calder

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Cinder Path
programme.desc: Feature-length drama about one man's journey through love and war in the early part of the 20th century. Charlie MacFell returns from university to the family farm, where his strict father still rules with an iron fist - a situation that soon changes thanks to a misunderstanding with a foolish farmhand. Lloyd Owen stars, with Catherine Zeta-Jones, Tom Bell and Maria Mile

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is seconded to a maternity ward, and is impressed by how modern technology is being used to help the mothers - but is left with a moral dilemma after assisting on a traumatic birth. A young man from Poplar is delighted to be accepted into university, but his mother is less than happy. His dreams are jeopardised when his girlfriend gets pregnant, a development that puts pressure on the whole family

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner and Shelagh are at the forefront of the advances in medical technology, and begin a campaign to ensure better respiratory health for their patients. However, Timothy fears their own lifestyle is not good for them and tries to encourage his parents to give up smoking. Violet injures her back and is forced to leave the shop in Fred's hands, a decision that strengthens their relationship but which proves to have other, unforeseen consequences. A new mother's past comes to light, with devastating repercussions for her family. Nurse Crane draws on her own experiences to help her

programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part drama. Martin Shaw stars alongside Janie Dee, Michael Maloney, Nicholas Le Prevost and Samantha Bond

programme.desc: It's Christmas Eve 1969 and Gently is enjoying a spot of festive cheer until he gets the news that a bank has been hit by armed robbers and he and Bacchus have work to do. Detective drama set in the north-east of England, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with a guest appearance by Jody Latham (Shameless)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A 79-year-old woman with dementia runs away from her care home and turns up at a south London police station to report a murder - sparking a reinvestigation into the mysterious disappearance of her police officer husband in 1956. The official report says that he fell into the river after one too many beers - but what secrets was this seemingly straight-as-a die old-fashioned bobby keeping? Meanwhile, Steve's son is arrested for buying weed, bringing the retired cop face to face with his ex-wife - a woman he hasn't seen in 10 years. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star, with a guest appearance from Julie Graham

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Leo, Nikki and Jack travel to war-torn Afghanistan to identify whether human remains are those of a missing British soldier suspected of being captured by Taliban forces five years earlier. However, they soon discover there is more at stake than identifying a soldier's remains as the crime scene is preventing the completion of a charity-run sanitation plant, putting thousands of lives at risk from contaminated water. programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebutt hosts a special live celebration of the Jewish festival of Passover. He is joined by special guests including Rob Rinder, Rebecca Front, Lesley Joseph and Rachel Stevens sharing their Passover traditions. There's great recipes from chefs Sarit Packer and Itamar Srulovich, and Helen McGinn will wake us all up to Kosher wines with her expert drink matches. Plus, great moments from the BBC food archive including The Hairy Bikers' visit to Israel

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley introduces a service for Palm Sunday from All Saints Church in Hale Barns. Sarah Small reflects on what Palm Sunday might mean today

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having visited Australia in a camper van 20 years ago, Emma Howells wants to move her family Down Under. However, husband Gerald is less sure. A trial week gives them the chance to see what life could be like for them in Perth, but will it give the couple answers or drive them further apart? Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant are on hand as two teams test their antiques knowledge at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire. Eric Knowles hosts

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: On Palm Sunday, Aled Jones visits Oxford to celebrate stories of hope, meeting a scientist behind the Covid-19 vaccine. YolanDa Brown meets musicians helping exhausted doctors

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Rudolph Walker appeals on behalf of the Stroke Association, a charity working to help people rebuild their lives after stroke

programme.title: My Mum Tracy Beaker
programme.desc: Jess and her mother move in with a famous footballer, but the lifestyle of a millionaire has an impact on the Beaker family. Feature-length episode of the children's drama

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers come up with designs for a social space in Corby, Northamptonshire, and a beach garden in Lee-on-the-Solent, Hampshire

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley discovers how science is helping better understand nature on Bedfordshire's Greensand Ridge, where he reports on the world's longest running bird survey, helps to map the levels of plastic pollution, and joins the Toad Patrol creating a new national amphibian DNA database. Tom Heap investigates the UK's environmental ambitions, and Adam Henson provides a spring update on his farm

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Christchurch Park in Ipswich, Suffolk, where treasures include an Imperial Chinese porcelain vase fit for an emperor and some intriguing portraits related to the Profumo scandal of the 1960s. Also featured are intricate, solid gold lapel badges from the Australian gold fields and a pair of delicate paintings by a famous Suffolk artist

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head on an icy expedition across the wild Scottish Highlands in the new Land Rover Defender, Mercedes G-Wagen and Ariel Nomad to see which vehicle will prove itself the ultimate off-roader

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Having opened an official inquiry into DCI Jo Davidson and Operation Lighthouse, AC-12 begin to suspect a cover up. Steve and Chloe dig into Gail Vella's controversial reporting for clues to her murder, and find links to previous AC-12 cases. Their investigation takes a shocking turn when they begin to scrutinise Jo's personal life

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of award-winning novelist Kazuo Ishiguro, who discusses his early life in Nagasaki and howe growing up in the home counties in the 1960s shaped his viewpoint as a writer. He also discusses his youthful desire to be a singer-songwriter, and chats about the music that has had a particular impact on his work. With contributions from writers Hanya Yanagihara and Bernardine Evaristo

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester United v West Ham United, Tottenham Hotspur v Arsenal and Chelsea v Aston Villa

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley hosts this property-based guessing game in which four people claim to own the same house. It's then up to celebrity judges Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix - joined each week by a special guest - to watch the four 'owners' interact and figure out which one is telling the truth and who are the impostors. Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen joins in the fun for the first edition, set in a converted barn in Ashford, Kent

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: New crimefighting tactics and technology, including a look at cameras with artificial intelligence. Plus, a burglar is caught breaking into an elderly woman's house

programme.desc: With the help of Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine the Reds and Blues battle it out at the Ardingly International Antiques Fair. Charlie Ross discovers the history behind the Kimono

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Miranda arrives at the Mill, impressing everyone but Valerie, who remains suspicious of her. Rob has to solve the mystery behind a violent attack on a pensioner

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the Monmouthshire countryside to help a couple who have a love of the outdoors and dogs, and hope to buy their first home together with a £700,000 budget

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal oversees the action as sisters Elsa and Gabriella pair up with chefs Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis to prepare a meal in 20 minutes

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team takes on a Victorian iron rocking chair, a mechanical musical bear ornament, and the battered and burned leather collar of an alpine cowbell

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray tries to make amends with Whitney by inviting her to a work gala, even lending her one of Chantelle's dresses to wear. Sharon tells Phil she wants in on his money laundering scheme and Suki scorns Kheerat for not showing Vinny the gratitude he deserves for stealing the gold bars. Mick teaching Frankie to drive, only for the car to break down

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Prudish Basil resorts to increasingly desperate measures to prevent an unmarried couple lowering moral standards at his hotel. Unfortunately, as his efforts become increasingly frantic, he succeeds only in making a fool of himself. Classic comedy, starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales, Andrew Sachs and Connie Booth

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Five returning contestants have to prepare a dish they would want on the menu of their own restaurant. Guest judge Anna Haugh is on hand to assess their efforts, resulting in two eliminations before the remaining chefs prepare a meal for the staff of Michelin-starred restaurant the Fat Duck, under the watchful eye of former executive chef Jonny Lake

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: Documentary following boxer Amir Khan and his fashion and beauty influencer wife Faryal Makhdoom. Faryal escapes the chaos at home for some work and leaves Amir to look after the kids, while the couple discuss making headlines and reflect on their past during a rare date night.

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: Faryal seeks therapy to address their relationships issues and past cheating allegations, whilst Amir plans a not-so-surprise birthday party for her in Dubai

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head on an icy expedition across the wild Scottish Highlands in the new Land Rover Defender, Mercedes G-Wagen and Ariel Nomad to see which vehicle will prove itself the ultimate off-roader

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Liam Neeson and Micheal Richardson, who are playing father and son in comedy-drama Made in Italy. Breakout star of The Serpent, Tahar Rahim, talks about his gripping new legal drama The Mauritanian, while Cush Jumbo reflects on five-part thriller The Beast Must Die, in which he stars. Comedian and actor Rob Brydon and the undisputed queen of Dragons' Den Deborah Meaden also feature. With music by singer-songwriter Laura Mvula

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Sally Nugent and Dan Walker present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts arrives in Willenhall, West Midlands, to see a semi-detached house. Lucy Alexander is at an overgrown plot of land in west London to find out the ambitious plans of the new owner, while Dion Dublin is in Liverpool to see a particularly colourful property

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Footage from a police body camera helps convict a violent offender who attacked a policewoman and the owners of a wine bar use facial recognition cameras to tackle pickpocketing

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Paisley crewmates Pete and Gary attend to an elderly patient who has collapsed at a supermarket. In Glasgow, John and Suzanne need to work out why a patient has collapsed at home

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from the Isle of Anglesey in north Wales, where experts Danny Sebastian and Nick Hall advise two competing teams anxious top make maximum profit at auction. Anita also meets up with gold prospector Vincent Thurkettle, who struck gold just off the nearby coast

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al is keen to try out his new game app on the team, and Karen proves especially enthusiastic. Emma is challenged when a stabbing leads to an internal police investigation

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton travels to the Warwickshire countryside to help a couple with a £600,000 budget find a home for them and their two young daughters

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Food obsessives Lily and Milly are best friends but today they become rivals as they bring in the ingredients for chefs Anna Haugh and Ellis Barrie to serve up the best dish

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers work their magic on a ride-on toy snail, a ballroom trophy that has lost its shine and a vintage bicycle given to a young girl by her father

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lily cannot understand why more is not being done to help her mum, so when Will mentions how Honey's injury brought his family together, it gives the distressed young girl an idea. Sharon considers moving into the fitness industry, while Mick receives an unwelcome phone call

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Cameron's comeuppance, Nicky vents her frustrations on a patient - and is forced to ask herself whether she really wants to raise a killer's baby. Reyhan challenges a traumatised Hanssen to tell Sahira the truth, but can he do it knowing it will destroy her? The AAU staff are surprised to see Ange back in scrubs so soon after her ordeal

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: An oil rig worker from Tyne and Wear is rushed to the A&E department of Gilbert Bain Hospital with a suspected stroke

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The return of Kay Mellor's lottery-based drama, this time following the workers at Woodvale Kennels, whose future looks in doubt when the owners announce the business is being sold to a large corporate chain. The only lifeline for gambling addict Keeley is the work's lottery, so when she checks the ticket with local newsagent Frank, she is ecstatic to learn they have won £500. But the team soon have reason to believe Frank may have made a mistake. Now all they need to do is find him. Starring Neil Morrissey, Katherine Rose Morley, Taj Atwal and Emily Head

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting how British Jews from across the faith celebrate life's big milestones, from birth to coming of age, marriage to the end of life. A 12-year-old prepares for his bar mitzvah, a couple marry in Spain, a modern ceremony they have adapted according to their beliefs, and at Bushey Cemetery in Hertfordshire, a woman describes her role as a volunteer who does the tahara, the ritual washing of the deceased before burial

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: A look at how British Hindus mark life's big milestones. In south London, Bhumi and Chirag are marking the birth of their six-day-old son with a traditional ceremony at home, inviting the gods to write his future. Meanwhile, when 18-year-old Samahit leaves for university, his family travel with him to bless his new home at the halls of residence

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman struggles to cope after Miss Watson's death, while Bradley goes to extreme lengths to find her father's killer. Prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Nicola Peltz

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: As Norman's obsession with Miss Watson continues, Norma tries to distract his thoughts elsewhere. Meanwhile, Bradley faces problems and needs assistance, and Romero is under pressure to make an arrest. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Max Thieriot

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at themes beginning with the letter C, as part of her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin helps volunteers in Lytham, Lancashire, with their entry as they aim to decorate 50 per cent of their seaside town with flowers and create a garden with a musical theme

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison celebrates the arrival of spring on her home turf in the Cotswolds. From the millions of snowdrops in Painswick's Rococo Gardens to discovering some old folklore, finding out about a scheme to enable wheelchair users to enjoy tougher terrain in the great outdoors and meeting the cattle that encourage wildflowers, Ellie also raids the Countryfile Archive, revisiting the time she went on dolphin watch in Cardigan Bay, Matt Baker visited tulip fields in Lincolnshire, and Adam Henson went to North Yorkshire to catch his supper

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don makes the most of the clocks going forward as he begins to sow dahlia seeds for summer flowers. He also reveals a new project, and shows what to plant now in the vegetable garden. Adam Frost extends the planting in his gravel garden and reveals his plans for the coming year

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Christine Walkden heads to Aberdeenshire in her hot-air balloon as her journey continues. She visits Crathes Castle, an ancient seat of Scottish nobles, where she learns about the challenges of gardening in an unforgiving climate, before meeting Doug, whose father left his mark on the estate. She also gets to grips with some smelly skunk cabbage at what is claimed to be Britain's highest garden, Tillypronie

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway walks along a stunning - and often overlooked - stretch of coastline, dominated by the Second Severn Crossing or Prince of Wales Bridge. On his journey, he rings an ancient church bell, finds a water scorpion that breathes through its bottom, learns about the Rogiet Hoard and discovers how the village of Sudbrook evolved. He also wades into the River Severn, following in the footsteps of ancient fisherman, and finishes in Chepstow at the start - or end - of the Wales Coast Path

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef visits the town of Madurai in Tamil Nadu, where he learns the art of temple cooking and samples south Indian dish sambar. He then takes a road trip across the Western Ghats to the state of Kerala, where he prepares regional dessert payasam and a traditional pork curry

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from the Central region go head to head and cook their six-course menus for panel of judges and Britain's first astronaut, Helen Sharman. How will she judge dishes celebrating British science and innovation, including two dishes paying homage to Professor Stephen Hawking's theory of black holes?

programme.title: Mary Berry's Easter Feast
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mary explores the roots of her faith through the medium of food, asking how Britain eats its way through Easter, and sharing her own favourite Easter recipes, including hot cross buns, Simnel cake and roast lamb. In this first episode, she discovers the traditions followed by diverse British families and communities during Lent and Good Friday, and the food that brings them together as each prepare for their Easter celebrations

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and chef Anna Haugh sample a luxurious dish originally created for Charles II, and explore the ingredients that went into one lavish dish at a royal banquet

programme.title: A Star Is Born (1954)
programme.desc: A Hollywood movie star with a drinking problem is captivated by an aspiring singer's performance. He falls in love with her and helps her become a star, but as her career blossoms, his falls into ruin. Musical drama, starring Judy Garland, James Mason, Jack Carson and Charles Bickford. Directed by George Cukor, with songs by Harold Arlen and Ira Gershwin

programme.title: Figure Skating
programme.desc: Kat Downes introduces action from the annual event, which this year took place at Ericsson Globe in Stockholm, Sweden. With analysis by Robin Cousins

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across Central America, from the Caribbean to the Pacific. Along the way, he goes diving on a coral reef on the coast of Belize, and witnesses a gun battle between park rangers and intruders in Chiquibul National Park. He also discovers how climate change has left Guatemala devastated by famine and drought, reports on measures to curb gang violence in El Salvador, and learns how Costa Rica aims to become the world's first carbon neutral country by 2021

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: Chris takes a look at nature's best navigators, with mule deer building up complicated memory maps of their landscapes so they don't get lost, while elephants rely on the knowledge of the herd's matriarch to find precious waterholes. Dung beetles plot routes by reading the stars, while the mantis shrimp has one of the most complex navigation systems in the natural world. For many species of fish and birds, there's safety in numbers and they often travel in vast shoals and flocks - but who leads the way, and how do they decide?

programme.title: Brazil with Michael Palin
programme.desc: Michael begins the third leg of his journey in the mineral-rich state of Minas Gerais, where he learns about the Brazilian mining industry and meets some of the people dedicated to overturning the environmental damage it causes. He then heads to Rio de Janeiro, focus of the next Olympics, where the authorities are ridding the streets of violent drug gangs that have controlled the city's shanty towns. On a lighter note, the globe-trotting broadcaster also learns to celebrate a goal like a well-known radio commentator and visits a 'love hotel'

programme.title: Frankie Goes to Russia
programme.desc: Part one of two. In a programme originally shown before the 2018 World Cup, Frankie Boyle explores Russia, looking at what lies behind national stereotypes and exploring the country's football culture. In the first episode, he visits the seaside town hosting the England team and a city renowned for football-related violence. He also joins a daredevil motorbike football team, records a world cup rap, and meets the sabre-wielding Cossacks policing the World Cup stadiums

programme.title: Frankie Goes to Russia
programme.desc: Part two of two. Frankie Boyle meets Russia's leading football historian in St Petersburg, and has a look round his collection of memorabilia. He attends a Spartak Moscow match in a corporate box, and considers how a team with proud proletarian roots has lost touch with its heritage. He also discovers that street footballers in Russia are able to make ends meet by selling their skills to advertisers, and ventures to a town where locals play a dangerous high-speed version of football on motorbikes

programme.title: The Men Who Stare at Goats (2009)
programme.desc: A journalist desperate to be posted to a war zone encounters a special forces agent, who claims to have been part of a top-secret project to endow soldiers with superhuman powers. As the reporter joins the supposed psychic on a journey across Iraq, he discovers the bizarre story behind the experiment. Satirical comedy, starring George Clooney, Ewan McGregor, Jeff Bridges and Kevin Spacey

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Noah and Tracy try to help a teenager who has suffered many hardships in life, and Matt is driven to extreme measures as Sylar continues to make trouble for him. Meanwhile, Claire and Gretchen are tormented by their new sorority sisters at Arlington University. Drama, starring Jack Coleman and Ali Larter

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Hiro travels three years into the past, hoping to save Charlie from being killed by Sylar - but Samuel's self-serving intervention complicates an already seemingly impossible task. Elsewhere, Noah's history with his Primatech partner Lauren is revealed. Glee's Jayma Mays guest stars

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from a virtual audience around the country

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Two members of staff find themselves in a tight spot, as Louis is bleeding out after being attacked by an old friend, while Cameron still has Ange held hostage - although the truth of his crimes is coming to light. Can he lie his way out of Holby or will those he has tricked manage to save their colleague end his reign of terror? Meanwhile, Dom returns to work after his operation and his decision to sue the hospital - and despite his attempts to forget the past, a petty patient soon revives his hunger for revenge

programme.title: Billy Monger's Big Red Nose Day Challenge
programme.desc: In April 2017 the professional racing driver was seriously injured in a collision that resulted in both of his legs being amputated. Already used to pushing the limits of what can be achieved, Billy now pushes himself to the limit as he takes on a truly unforgettable challenge - walking, kayaking and cycling 140 miles in the name of charity. His trek begins at the Millennium Bridge in Newcastle-upon-Tyne, ultimately heading to Brands Hatch in Kent. Before he starts, the 21-year-old receives words of encouragement from Jose Mourinho, manager of his beloved Tottenham Hotspur, and along the route is joined by a host of celebrity supporters including Cheryl, Zoe Ball, Chris Ramsey, Dan Walker and Vick Hope

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the competition from Heanor Antiques Centre in Derbyshire, where teams are guided by experts Catherine Southon and Thomas Forrester as they try to find three interesting items that might generate a profit at auction. Natasha also finds out about the value of provenance

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes with animals and keepers at Edinburgh Zoo and the Highland Wildlife Park, beginning as a new arrival joins the resident dik-dik family. World Rhino Day gives staff a chance to let their hair down and have some fun as they raise both money and awareness of conservation issues

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Operations manager Kate faces her first challenge, taking on money blogger Jordon on his fourth appearance. She's also up against veteran Andrew, a retired police officer marking his seventh time in the Head Hunter position. As usual, contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. Quiz show, hosted by Rob Beckett

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three detached properties in Leicestershire are in the spotlight, featuring a retro family home, a country-style new-build and a house with some seriously modern twists

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Zak, Ike and Steve are missing at sea and the town mounts a huge search-and-rescue operation, while Jan has gone into labour. Antipodean comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: Nature's Great Events
programme.desc: A lion family's struggle to survive in the Serengeti grasslands, where they have been left without a ready source of prey following the annual migration of wildebeest and zebras. Not only do the females have difficulty finding food for their young, but a fire destroys the cover they need to ambush the few animals that remain - and the number of cubs gradually dwindles to just two

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the hunting prowess of the most widespread of marine mammals - the dolphin. Contrary to its amiable reputation, it is in fact a ruthless predator, using a combination of specialised anatomy and complex communication to hunt. The programme also reveals how a pod employs body movements to disclose the location of food

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Highlights of the best finds by experts Anita Manning, Mark Stacey and others during the programme's travels around the country. Items featured include a set of Beatles autographs and a collection of Matchbox toys. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the birthplace of novelist and playwright DH Lawrence

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: Sabrina Grant hosts as people bring in loved-but-unused treasures to be transformed into contemporary and useful items by a team of expert crafters. Jane brings in her late mother's wedding dress and pink suede jacket, with metalcraft expert Leigh and seamstress Elizabeth pitching radical designs to her

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: The food writer shares ideas to make everyday family meals special with no-fuss dishes and also tries her hand at milking a goat before showing her granddaughter how to make her own goat's cheese for a family picnic

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Otto von Bismarck, the history of hand-knitting, Robert Louis Stevenson, and the films of the Coen Brothers, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell asks the questions in the final of the quiz, with two teams battling it out to succeed last year's winners the 007s and be named champions. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between groups of four things that may at first appear not to be linked, with one set of clues comprising cosy firearms, anxious puddings, demon existed, and canine deity

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second and concluding semi-final sees two teams of four students battling it out to make it to the climax of the competition and the chance to take home the trophy. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Finding Jack Charlton
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke presents a profile of the World Cup-winning footballer, looking at his special relationship with Ireland after becoming the national team's manager, his complicated relationship with brother Bobby, and his final battle with dementia. including contributions from his wife Pat and son John, alongside celebrated Irish players, musicians, writers and politicians, including Larry Mullen, Roddy Doyle, Brendan O'Carroll, Paul McGrath, Niall Quinn, Mick McCarthy, David O'Leary and Andy Townsend

programme.title: Afoot Again in the Past
programme.desc: The World Cup-winning footballer and former Republic of Ireland manager visits Warkworth Castle in Northumberland

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Match of Their Day
programme.desc: Garth Crooks talks to World Cup winner Jack Charlton about his playing career with Leeds United and England

programme.title: The Damned United (2009)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama chronicling Brian Clough's ill-fated 44-day tenure as boss of Leeds United in 1974, after Don Revie's departure for the job of England's manager. Following his acrimonious split from Derby County and a brief spell at Brighton & Hove Albion, Clough arrived at Elland Road on a wave of controversy, after previously being an outspoken critic of the club, and struggled to find allies among the playing staff. With Michael Sheen, Timothy Spall, Jim Broadbent and Colm Meaney

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison celebrates the arrival of spring on her home turf in the Cotswolds. From the millions of snowdrops in Painswick's Rococo Gardens to discovering some old folklore, finding out about a scheme to enable wheelchair users to enjoy tougher terrain in the great outdoors and meeting the cattle that encourage wildflowers, Ellie also raids the Countryfile Archive, revisiting the time she went on dolphin watch in Cardigan Bay, Matt Baker visited tulip fields in Lincolnshire, and Adam Henson went to North Yorkshire to catch his supper

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a man in Scotland whose online girlfriend in Ghana has demanded thousands of pounds, but has never visited him

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team transform an attic in the oldest house in the village of Wrington, North Somerset into a writer's retreat-themed B&B

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: New crimefighting tactics and technology, including a look at cameras with artificial intelligence. Plus, a burglar is caught breaking into an elderly woman's house

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don welcomes spring at Longmeadow with plenty of changes in the garden to share. As well as getting on with some essential pruning, he lifts and divides perennials, starts sowing chillies and begins growing this year's vegetables. Plus, Carol Klein shares the highlights of the plants that shine out in winter at Bressingham Gardens in Norfolk

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Rudolph Walker appeals on behalf of the Stroke Association, a charity working to help people rebuild their lives after stroke

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents highlights and analysis from the opening round of matches in the Super League season, including Castleford Tigers v Warrington Wolves, Hull FC v Huddersfield Giants and Leigh Centurions v Wigan Warriors

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The owners of a traditional Edwardian end-of-terrace, a Victorian terrace brought crashing into the 21st century, and a terrace that has been stretched to its limit all compete

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Jan needs to know who the father of her twins is, and Woody has a dark secret about his wedding. Shay is relieved to have Ike back, but the boat accident has taken its toll on him

programme.title: Nature's Great Events
programme.desc: David Attenborough describes the annual surge of more than 100 million sardines along South Africa's east coast as inshore waters cool with the arrival of winter. This marine spectacle provides a feast for competing predators including dolphins, whales, sharks and seabirds, but the sardine run has become less predictable in recent years - posing a threat to the creatures that depend on it to survive

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham examines the senses, anatomy and physiology of bats to understand what enables the creatures to thrive in surprising places. Tiny hairs on their wings give them a detailed air-flow map during flight, while heat sensors on the nose of vampire bats allow them to search out the most blood-rich areas of a prey's body, and iron oxide particles in the bat brain may act as a compass, enabling them to find the most direct route back to the roost

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Caroline Hawley and Nick Hall are on hand at the City Hall in Hull, hoping to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Caroline is delighted to find a painting by local artist Steven Dews and Nick is bedazzled by some iconic 1970s jewellery. Presenter Paul Martin visits the Wilberforce House Museum in Hull's Old Town to hear the story of Hull-born William Wilberforce, the great anti-slave trade campaigner

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: Loved-but-unused treasures are transformed into contemporary and useful items by a team of expert crafters. This time, Xinhua arrives at the workshop with a Chinese dowry box, which she now uses to keep her make-up, and carpentry expert Shelley and metal specialist John offer radically different designs to choose from. Elsewhere, other items include an old family tent and a single bowling pin

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: For the fourth leg of their journey, Dave Myers and Si King travel from Amarillo, Texas, to Albuquerque in New Mexico. In Amarillo, the pair visit the oldest burger joint in the city, before riding to Palo Duro Canyon to explore a ghost town. Finally arriving in Albuquerque, they join residents to watch a game at the local ballpark and bake a chili chocolate pie to celebrate their cross-country adventure

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Another host of leading food experts take Fred Sirieix to their favourite restaurants to explore what makes them so special. In this opening episode, Nadiya Hussain takes Fred to visit some of her beloved eateries around Yorkshire. Whilst on their journey, the pair sample traditional bakes, world-class cheese and spicy samosas

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down on a busy residential street in Manchester, a team of specialist GMP detectives race to identify the masked men responsible

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sindhu Vee hosts this episode of the comedy showcase recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, featuring stand-up performances by New York-based Edinburgh comedy award winner Alex Edelman, and at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe Comedians' Choice Award winner Lou Sanders

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: Chris takes a look at nature's best navigators, with mule deer building up complicated memory maps of their landscapes so they don't get lost, while elephants rely on the knowledge of the herd's matriarch to find precious waterholes. Dung beetles plot routes by reading the stars, while the mantis shrimp has one of the most complex navigation systems in the natural world. For many species of fish and birds, there's safety in numbers and they often travel in vast shoals and flocks - but who leads the way, and how do they decide?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to the Lake District for an emotional reunion with their own dads' old cars. Elsewhere, Chris heads out on track to test Lamborghini's most powerful car ever, the hybrid Sian

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace present the latest heat, as five more hopefuls get their first chance to win an apron by cooking their Signature Dish - something that tells John and Gregg about who they are as a cook and how good they can become. After tasting all five plates, John and Gregg pick their favourite dishes, and those cooks will earn themselves not only a MasterChef apron but also a place in the next round

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and his team travel to rural Oxfordshire to meet Asiyla and her family and help transform an old sailing boat into unique glamping accommodation.

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The secret lives of the wildlife in the popular Cornish tourist attraction, set against the background of the gardening year. Charlie Hamilton James shoots footage highlighting a variety of creatures inhabiting the estate, including foxes at play in the pleasure gardens after the visitors have left, toads mating and the badgers that enter the tea rooms at night. Narrated by Philippa Forrester

programme.title: Brotherhood: The Inner Life of Monks
programme.desc: Documentary about Mount St Bernard Abbey, a monastery with a population of just 25 monks, half of whom are over 80, and which will soon be home to the first Trappist brewery in the UK - a venture that is essential to the brotherhood's survival. The monks reflect on spirituality, ageing and the end of life, as the number of burials in the abbey graveyard continues to grow

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The naturalist investigates the impact of a growing human population on the world in light of predictions that the number of humans could reach 10 billion by the year 2050. He considers the steps that will need to be taken for Earth's natural resources to support so many people, visiting a city in Brazil that is on the verge of running out of water and a community school in Nigeria that may present a possible solution. Featuring contributions from leading environmental experts including David Attenborough

programme.title: Between the Lines
programme.desc: First episode of the 1990s police drama, starring Neil Pearson as a police officer investigating corruption on the force. Tony Clark is ordered to investigate allegations of widespread corruption at his own station, and begins by focusing on a group of officers believed to be profiting from the laundering of drug money. With Tom Georgeson and Siobhan Redmond

programme.title: Between the Lines
programme.desc: Armed police officers shoot a local youth, stirring up strong feelings among some sections of the community and leading to fears of violent reprisals. Gritty drama, starring Neil Pearson, Tom Georgeson and Siobhan Redmond

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at television appearances made over the years by the actor and writer Dirk Bogarde, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms, who starred with him in the 1961 film Victim

programme.title: Count Basie Through His Own Eyes
programme.desc: Hitherto, little has been known about Count Basie's private and family life, but here director Jeremy Marre offers up a treasure trove of home movies and photo albums that show the bandleader and pianist's remarkable relationship with his wife Catherine - whose pioneering support for African-American causes placed her at the side of Martin Luther King - and disabled daughter Diane. Through Basie's intimate footage and letters, this film reveals the passions and ambitions that inspired the world-famous musician. Contributors incluide his friends Quincy Jones and Annie Ross

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The secret lives of the wildlife in the popular Cornish tourist attraction, set against the background of the gardening year. Charlie Hamilton James shoots footage highlighting a variety of creatures inhabiting the estate, including foxes at play in the pleasure gardens after the visitors have left, toads mating and the badgers that enter the tea rooms at night. Narrated by Philippa Forrester

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The naturalist investigates the impact of a growing human population on the world in light of predictions that the number of humans could reach 10 billion by the year 2050. He considers the steps that will need to be taken for Earth's natural resources to support so many people, visiting a city in Brazil that is on the verge of running out of water and a community school in Nigeria that may present a possible solution. Featuring contributions from leading environmental experts including David Attenborough

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The explorer digs out his rucksack and heads for Windermere in the Lake District. The National Park attracts 18 million visitors each year, and is now a World Heritage site. Here, Paul finds out why the early tourists thought the Lakes was one of the most dangerous spots in the country. He also plunges into the world of long-distance swimming and helps the staff who run the boats on the lake and meets a Holocaust survivor who was airlifted to Windermere in 1945 as part of a mercy mission involving 300 orphans after the Second World War

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American painter Bob Ross demonstrates how to create the illusion of a mountain on a bright day mirrored in still waters

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould examine a still life that may have been painted by prolific artist William Nicholson, but which has been rejected by leading experts on his work. They discover a useful source of evidence in Nicholson's own paint box, but as they delve into the painting's history, they discover it may have been connected to one of the 20th century's greatest art crimes. Fiona meets a reformed forger to discover if he ever faked a Nicholson while Philip takes the painting to Canada to compare it to another of the artist's works

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about a fraud scandal in Romania's healthcare system that was exposed after survivors of a nightclub fire began dying in suspicious circumstances while recovering in hospital with non-life-threatening injuries. When a new health minister was subsequently appointed, journalists follow his attempts, in the face of monumental obstacles, to reform a system riddled with corruption

programme.title: Art of France
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines the history of French art, revealing how it emerged from a struggle between tradition and revolution, and rulers and citizens. Starting with the invention of Gothic architecture, he traces its development up until the arrival of Classicism and the Age of Enlightenment

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Michael Mosley uses footage from the BBC archives to chart scientists' progress as they looked at the mind of a murderer in an attempt to understand why people commit such crimes, asking whether they are driven to it by circumstances or are simply born to kill

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American painter Bob Ross demonstrates how to create the illusion of a mountain on a bright day mirrored in still waters

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The explorer heads to Derwentwater and Borrowdale, a region of England susceptible to extreme weather, where he meets some of the locals who have battled against flooding and learned to live with the conditions. Along the way, Paul tells the story of Millican Dalton, the 'Professor of Adventure', who ran his own expeditions in the Lake District more than 70 years ago. He also heads for the summit of Great Gable, one of the Lake's best-known peaks

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a seascape with a dramatic sky that marks an approaching storm, setting the mood with a canvas painted half black and half white

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Jim Hacker believes he needs to know everything going on in his department, and is furious to learn Sir Humphrey is withholding information. However, when events take a surprising turn, he starts to understand why sometimes it is better to keep a minister in the dark

programme.title: The Fall and Rise of Reginald Perrin
programme.desc: Reggie changes nationality several times in his search for a new identity, but gradually finds himself drawn back to his old life. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Treasures of the Anglo-Saxons
programme.desc: Historian Dr Janina Ramirez investigates the development of Anglo-Saxon art. Examining the Sutton Hoo treasures the Franks Casket, the Staffordshire Hoard and the Lindisfarne Gospels, she uncovers the secret codes and symbols that reveal the pagan past and Christian future of the Anglo-Saxon people, and discusses how their artistic development was brought to an end by the Norman invasion of 1066

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The stories of murder investigations and their consequences, which overturned laws, transformed police interrogation and revolutionised forensic detection. It begins with the hunt to find the killer of two teenage girls in 1980s Leicestershire, which led to an extraordinary forensic breakthrough - DNA fingerprinting

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Emlyn, is a 13-year-old sheep farmer who lives in Wales. All year round, alongside his dog Patch, Emlyn is responsible for over a thousand sheep, helping them to deliver healthy baby lambs that will allow the family to survive. As a bitter winter hits the farm, hundreds of lambs' lives are at risk and Emlyn rushes to help them pull through. He's following in a long tradition - his family has been farming for more than 150 years - but tragedy struck when Emlyn was four years old when his father died. Now he takes responsibility for helping his mum to keep the farm going, which is crucial for the family's survival. Emlyn combines life as a secondary school student with the demands of a busy sheep farm. He's up at 6.30 every morning, touring the farm on his quadbike before breakfast, and then again for a few hours after school. There are always jobs to do from rescuing sheep that have escaped to spreading manure on the fields, fixing fences and feeding the sheep. As the year progresses Em

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Erin Ranney is a wildlife camera operator and adventurer. She splits her time between Alaska and the Falklands and loves filming the planet’s most incredible wildlife. Erin’s mission is to film rockhopper penguins on the Falkland Islands and find out why these charismatic creatures are facing serious threats to their survival. She investigates why the rockhopper population has fallen by 80 per cent since the 1930s. Climate change is the biggest threat to the rockhoppers, causing violent storms that could see them penguins face extinction. Erin also finds out how planting tussac grass can combat climate change and discovers that what we do in our own countries can affect these penguins thousands of miles away. She talks about how we can all make a difference towards helping the planet, just by taking small steps

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Jahawi is an underwater camera operator who is passionate about preserving the coastline of Kenya, his home country. The ocean is home to a whole host of marine wildlife, but one species here is particularly under threat: dolphins. This fun and curious mammal is facing a number of issues, as their habitat is being destroyed and the fish they eat are being depleted. There is also significant human activity on this coastline, which is damaging the marine life. However, Lamu has stood the test of time and is a marine sanctuary for them, with a new port being built and things about to change. Working with the Kenyan Marine Mammal Network, Jahawi is hoping to get the area protected as a marine reserve before it’s too late. It doesn’t take long for Jahawi to find the resident bottle-nosed dolphins as they enjoy swimming at the bow of the boat, and he soon gets into the water with the dolphins to get a closer look at these magnificent creatures. here is one species of dolphin he is hoping to

programme.desc: Suri's family are from Iran, a large country in the Middle East in the continent of Asia. She makes the traditional Iranian dish of chicken joojeh. She then takes her special ingredient of saffron, grinds it down to a powder, and adds salt and natural yoghurt before she mixes it in with the rest of the marinade. Will her friends like the special ingredient when it is cooked in her joojeh?

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie on an adventure around the world of flowers to see how we can help to look after them and our planet. Find out how flowers and different animals, like bees, birds, butterflies and insects, work together. We learn how bees and insects pollinate flowers, as Maddie becomes a bee and plays the pollination game! Along the way, we’ll discover flowers of all different shapes, sizes and colours and learn why they are so different. We discover the different parts of a flower, the stigma, the anther, the stamen and the petals and how some flowers go to sleep at night and open in the day!

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Casey the Badger has a taste for adventure. And today is no different! He’s heard there might be monsters in the field nearby, and he just can’t resist going to explore. But who’ll go with him? Maybe Rosie the Rabbit? Or his brothers and sisters? No one wants to join him, so he goes by himself. Wrapped up in all the fun, he ends up far away and completely lost. Just how will he get back home?

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: We learn about objects that are associated with the colour black, from blackbirds to blackberries, as well as explore how black patterns are used. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour black.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Animation following the adventure of a group of animals living on a patchwork blanket

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Matilda, Samuel and their families as they discover the world around them at two months old. Both children head out for the day with their families. Samuel goes on a relaxing walk with his mum and dad in the nearby park, whilst Matilda goes to a different park with her family to feed the ducks and watch the graceful swans. After heading home with their families, Samuel plays in his swing with mum. Matilda has a soothing, warm bath before being breastfed and rocked slowly to sleep. Samuel is also getting tired, so mum and dad run him a warm bath to make sure he gets cleaned up, before giving him his bottle of milk. The next morning, Matilda’s family are all in bed together as she gives Callum a big hug! Samuel is also feeling refreshed and ready to have some cuddles with mum before breakfast.

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: Alexandra shows us her barrister’s wig. She explains why she loves her wig so much and what it means to be able to wear it.

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: Team Tinpo are tidying up their HQ and testing out a bouncy new invention

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Diellza's family comes from Kosovo. She makes a traditional recipe of stuffed peppers. Diellza has lovely colourful ingredients for her stuffed peppers such as spring onions, sweetcorn, grated carrot. She also uses cooked rice, chopped tomatoes, parsley, garlic puree and paprika for seasoning. This yummy filling is stuffed into her peppers and placed in the oven to be cooked for her friends. To accompany her meal, Diellza serves her friends a traditional Kosovan drink made of yoghurt and water called kos. Will they enjoy her drink, and will they guess what the special ingredient is?

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie on a journey of discovery to find out about plants around the world. Find out how we can help to look after them and our planet, and why and how they grow and survive in different climates. Maddie explores plants in the rainforest, big and small, and sees how the clever drip-tip leaves work watering the plants beneath them. We’ll see how plants in hot, dry parts of world like the desert thrive in their own special ways, like the cactus plant that stores litres of water in its waxy leaves. And we’ll learn how plants that grow close to home in the UK, can grow in a colder and wetter climate.

There's great recipes from chefs Sarit Packer and Itamar Srulovich, and Helen McGinn will wake us all up to Kosher wines with her expert drink matches. Plus, great moments from the BBC food archive including The Hairy Bikers' visit to Israel

programme.desc: As Aled Jones turns 50, this special programme looks back at his life and celebrates his musical career since making a name for himself as a young soprano. Throughout the programme, Aled discovers archive of himself as a boy he had no idea existed, and also reflects on some of the highlights of his career, singing for Pope John Paul, the Royal family and at the wedding of Bob Geldof and Paula Yates

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley discovers how science is helping better understand nature on Bedfordshire's Greensand Ridge, where he reports on the world's longest running bird survey, helps to map the levels of plastic pollution, and joins the Toad Patrol creating a new national amphibian DNA database. Tom Heap investigates the UK's environmental ambitions, and Adam Henson provides a spring update on his farm

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Christchurch Park in Ipswich, Suffolk, where treasures include an Imperial Chinese porcelain vase fit for an emperor and some intriguing portraits related to the Profumo scandal of the 1960s. Also featured are intricate, solid gold lapel badges from the Australian gold fields and a pair of delicate paintings by a famous Suffolk artist

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head on an icy expedition across the wild Scottish Highlands in the new Land Rover Defender, Mercedes G-Wagen and Ariel Nomad to see which vehicle will prove itself the ultimate off-roader

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Having opened an official inquiry into DCI Jo Davidson and Operation Lighthouse, AC-12 begin to suspect a cover up. Steve and Chloe dig into Gail Vella's controversial reporting for clues to her murder, and find links to previous AC-12 cases. Their investigation takes a shocking turn when they begin to scrutinise Jo's personal life

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: The deaths of three generations of one family in June 1999, a brutal mass murder that shocked the close-knit community of Clydach, near Swansea. Wyre Davies reports on new evidence

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of award-winning novelist Kazuo Ishiguro, who discusses his early life in Nagasaki and howe growing up in the home counties in the 1960s shaped his viewpoint as a writer. He also discusses his youthful desire to be a singer-songwriter, and chats about the music that has had a particular impact on his work. With contributions from writers Hanya Yanagihara and Bernardine Evaristo

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray tries to make amends with Whitney by inviting her to a work gala, even lending her one of Chantelle's dresses to wear. Mick teaching Frankie to drive, only for the car to break down

Reyhan challenges a traumatised Hanssen to tell Sahira the truth, but can he do it knowing it will destroy her? The AAU staff are surprised to see Ange back in scrubs so soon after her ordeal

programme.title: Nightshifters
programme.desc: Stuart has been working night shifts at Tata Steelworks in Port Talbot for almost 20 years, Sian is on the late-night taxi shift in Swansea, while in Cardiff, artist Nathan is melting chocolate in a microwave prior to creating a portrait of Lord Alan Sugar using sweets and confectionery. In this continuing series revealing the hidden world of night-time workers, all three reveal their very different reasons for working at night

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The return of Kay Mellor's lottery-based drama, this time following the workers at Woodvale Kennels, whose future looks in doubt when the owners announce the business is being sold to a large corporate chain. The only lifeline for gambling addict Keeley is the work's lottery, so when she checks the ticket with local newsagent Frank, she is ecstatic to learn they have won £500. But the team soon have reason to believe Frank may have made a mistake. Now all they need to do is find him. Starring Neil Morrissey, Katherine Rose Morley, Taj Atwal and Emily Head

programme.title: Match of the Day Wales
programme.desc: Wales v Czech Republic. Jason Mohammad presents highlights of the World Cup qualifier at Cardiff City Stadium. Studio guests include former Wales international Joe Ledley and ex-Wales manager Jayne Ludlow

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting how British Jews from across the faith celebrate life's big milestones, from birth to coming of age, marriage to the end of life. programme.desc: Religious thoughts from Scotland, plus a selection of hymns

programme.title: Sunday Worship

Plus, great moments from the BBC food archive including The Hairy Bikers' visit to Israel

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Carl from Stoke brings in a key that turns out to be not quite what it appears. Naz from Halifax presents a harmonica that she wants to sell so she and her young son can have a holiday, and Martin from London tries to get rid of a piece of furniture that holds a hidden secret

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: On Palm Sunday, Aled Jones visits Oxford to celebrate stories of hope, meeting a scientist behind the Covid-19 vaccine. YolanDa Brown meets musicians helping exhausted doctors

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Rudolph Walker appeals on behalf of the Stroke Association, a charity working to help people rebuild their lives after stroke

programme.title: My Mum Tracy Beaker
programme.desc: Jess and her mother move in with a famous footballer, but the lifestyle of a millionaire has an impact on the Beaker family. Feature-length episode of the children's drama

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Metal expert Dominic Chinea and wood restorer Will Kirk join forces on a wooden travelling trunk, which was used by a family of Polish refugees as they travelled across continents looking for a new home after the Second World War. Steve Fletcher has trouble getting a wobbly toy cyclist back on the straight and narrow, Brenton West tries to make an old bell ring again and Kirsten Ramsay is asked to repair a damaged glass trophy, which a couple want to pass on in memory of their late daughter, the original recipient

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs and isolated for millions of years, leaving weird and wonderful animals found nowhere else on Earth. In its jungles, a cassowary - one of the most dangerous birds in the world - stands six feet tall, while kangaroos and wombats brave snowstorms, and gum-tree forests are filled with predators. In its red desert heart, reptiles drink through their skin and huge flocks of wild budgerigars swirl in search of water. On secret islands Tasmanian devils roam, and offshore thousands of sharks gather for a rare event

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley discovers how science is helping better understand nature on Bedfordshire's Greensand Ridge, where he reports on the world's longest running bird survey, helps to map the levels of plastic pollution, and joins the Toad Patrol creating a new national amphibian DNA database. Tom Heap investigates the UK's environmental ambitions, and Adam Henson provides a spring update on his farm

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Christchurch Park in Ipswich, Suffolk, where treasures include an Imperial Chinese porcelain vase fit for an emperor and some intriguing portraits related to the Profumo scandal of the 1960s. Also featured are intricate, solid gold lapel badges from the Australian gold fields and a pair of delicate paintings by a famous Suffolk artist

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head on an icy expedition across the wild Scottish Highlands in the new Land Rover Defender, Mercedes G-Wagen and Ariel Nomad to see which vehicle will prove itself the ultimate off-roader

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Having opened an official inquiry into DCI Jo Davidson and Operation Lighthouse, AC-12 begin to suspect a cover up. Steve and Chloe dig into Gail Vella's controversial reporting for clues to her murder, and find links to previous AC-12 cases. Their investigation takes a shocking turn when they begin to scrutinise Jo's personal life

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Israel v Scotland. Action from the qualifier at Bloomfield Stadium in Tel Aviv, as Steve Clarke's side continued their quest to make Qatar 2022

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of award-winning novelist Kazuo Ishiguro, who discusses his early life in Nagasaki and howe growing up in the home counties in the 1960s shaped his viewpoint as a writer. He also discusses his youthful desire to be a singer-songwriter, and chats about the music that has had a particular impact on his work. With contributions from writers Hanya Yanagihara and Bernardine Evaristo

programme.title: The One Show
