programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: Reporter Kevin Keane investigates what Scots really think about the change to our climate and asks whether we are able and willing to act now to save the planet, discovering how many people would be willing to give up driving, eat less meat, and install a more efficient heating system at home

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray tries to make amends with Whitney by inviting her to a work gala, even lending her one of Chantelle's dresses to wear. Sharon tells Phil she wants in on his money laundering scheme and Suki scorns Kheerat for not showing Vinny the gratitude he deserves for stealing the gold bars. Mick teaching Frankie to drive, only for the car to break down

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Prudish Basil resorts to increasingly desperate measures to prevent an unmarried couple lowering moral standards at his hotel. Unfortunately, as his efforts become increasingly frantic, he succeeds only in making a fool of himself. Classic comedy, starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales, Andrew Sachs and Connie Booth

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Five returning contestants have to prepare a dish they would want on the menu of their own restaurant. Guest judge Anna Haugh is on hand to assess their efforts, resulting in two eliminations before the remaining chefs prepare a meal for the staff of Michelin-starred restaurant the Fat Duck, under the watchful eye of former executive chef Jonny Lake

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: Documentary following boxer Amir Khan and his fashion and beauty influencer wife Faryal Makhdoom. Faryal escapes the chaos at home for some work and leaves Amir to look after the kids, while the couple discuss making headlines and reflect on their past during a rare date night.

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: Faryal seeks therapy to address their relationships issues and past cheating allegations, whilst Amir plans a not-so-surprise birthday party for her in Dubai

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head on an icy expedition across the wild Scottish Highlands in the new Land Rover Defender, Mercedes G-Wagen and Ariel Nomad to see which vehicle will prove itself the ultimate off-roader

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Liam Neeson and Micheal Richardson, who are playing father and son in comedy-drama Made in Italy. Breakout star of The Serpent, Tahar Rahim, talks about his gripping new legal drama The Mauritanian, while Cush Jumbo reflects on five-part thriller The Beast Must Die, in which he stars. Comedian and actor Rob Brydon and the undisputed queen of Dragons' Den Deborah Meaden also feature. With music by singer-songwriter Laura Mvula

programme.desc: Paisley crewmates Pete and Gary attend to an elderly patient who has collapsed at a supermarket. In Glasgow, John and Suzanne need to work out why a patient has collapsed at home

programme.desc: Footage from a police body camera helps convict a violent offender who attacked a policewoman and the owners of a wine bar use facial recognition cameras to tackle pickpocketing

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Paisley crewmates Pete and Gary attend to an elderly patient who has collapsed at a supermarket. In Glasgow, John and Suzanne need to work out why a patient has collapsed at home

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from the Isle of Anglesey in north Wales, where experts Danny Sebastian and Nick Hall advise two competing teams anxious top make maximum profit at auction. Anita also meets up with gold prospector Vincent Thurkettle, who struck gold just off the nearby coast

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al is keen to try out his new game app on the team, and Karen proves especially enthusiastic. Emma is challenged when a stabbing leads to an internal police investigation

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton travels to the Warwickshire countryside to help a couple with a £600,000 budget find a home for them and their two young daughters

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Food obsessives Lily and Milly are best friends but today they become rivals as they bring in the ingredients for chefs Anna Haugh and Ellis Barrie to serve up the best dish

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers work their magic on a ride-on toy snail, a ballroom trophy that has lost its shine and a vintage bicycle given to a young girl by her father

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lily cannot understand why more is not being done to help her mum, so when Will mentions how Honey's injury brought his family together, it gives the distressed young girl an idea. Sharon considers moving into the fitness industry, while Mick receives an unwelcome phone call

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Cameron's comeuppance, Nicky vents her frustrations on a patient - and is forced to ask herself whether she really wants to raise a killer's baby. Reyhan challenges a traumatised Hanssen to tell Sahira the truth, but can he do it knowing it will destroy her? The AAU staff are surprised to see Ange back in scrubs so soon after her ordeal

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: An oil rig worker from Tyne and Wear is rushed to the A&E department of Gilbert Bain Hospital with a suspected stroke

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The return of Kay Mellor's lottery-based drama, this time following the workers at Woodvale Kennels, whose future looks in doubt when the owners announce the business is being sold to a large corporate chain. The only lifeline for gambling addict Keeley is the work's lottery, so when she checks the ticket with local newsagent Frank, she is ecstatic to learn they have won £500. But the team soon have reason to believe Frank may have made a mistake. Now all they need to do is find him. Starring Neil Morrissey, Katherine Rose Morley, Taj Atwal and Emily Head

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Jim Fitzpatrick chairs a topical debate, in which a panel of studio guests face tough questions on the big issues of the week, posed directly by audience members

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting how British Jews from across the faith celebrate life's big milestones, from birth to coming of age, marriage to the end of life. A 12-year-old prepares for his bar mitzvah, a couple marry in Spain, a modern ceremony they have adapted according to their beliefs, and at Bushey Cemetery in Hertfordshire, a woman describes her role as a volunteer who does the tahara, the ritual washing of the deceased before burial

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: A look at how British Hindus mark life's big milestones. In south London, Bhumi and Chirag are marking the birth of their six-day-old son with a traditional ceremony at home, inviting the gods to write his future. Meanwhile, when 18-year-old Samahit leaves for university, his family travel with him to bless his new home at the halls of residence

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman struggles to cope after Miss Watson's death, while Bradley goes to extreme lengths to find her father's killer. Prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Nicola Peltz

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of House of Commons proceedings

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.desc: Highlights of the week in politics in the USA from C-SPAN

programme.desc: Highlights of the week from the USA

programme.desc: Interviews with newsmakers and personalities from across the globe

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: A review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: Senedd Cymru - Welsh Parliament
programme.desc: Economy and Transport Minister Lee Waters making a statement on the Welsh Government's trasnport strategy, from Tuesday 23 March

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from a virtual audience around the country

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Boris Johnson in front of the Liaison Committee, from Wednesday 24 March

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Environment, Food and Rural Affairs Committee's session on seafood and meat exports to the EU with evidence from Secretary of State, George Eustice, from Thursday 25 March

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Defence Committee's session on 'Defending Global Britain in a Competitive Age' with evidence from Lord Richards of Herstmonceux , Chief of Defence Staff (2010-13), and Lord Houghton of Richmond , Chief of Defence Staff (2014-16), from Tuesday 22 March

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Health and Social Care Committee session on children and young people's mental health, with evidence from Dame Rachel de Souza, children's commissioner for England, from Tuesday 23 March.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: National Security Strategy Joint Committee session on the machinery of national security, with evidence from former prime minister Theresa May and former foreign secretary and defence Secretary Lord Hammond of Runnymede, from Monday 22 March.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: National Security Strategy Joint Committee session on the machinery of national security, with evidence from former prime minister Theresa May and former foreign secretary and defence Secretary Lord Hammond of Runnymede, from Monday 22 March.

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of House of Commons proceedings

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint National Security Strategy Committee session on the UK's national security machinery, with evidence from former prime minister David Cameron and former national security advisor Peter Rickets, from Monday 1 March.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Former Governor of the Bank of England Mark Carney charts how financial value has come to be held in higher esteem than human value, beginning by assessing what value actually means. How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Despite losing the presidency and both Houses of Congress, Donald Trump still seems to have a chokehold on the Republican party. So what should Republican anti-Trumpers do now? Do they fight from within, or create a new Conservative Party? Stephen Sackur interviews Evan McMullin, one of their key organisers and strategists

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Former prime minister Tony Blair speaking at the Institute for Government on the last twelve months of the Covid-19 pandemic, its lasting impact and the challenges posed for politicians, from Friday 26 February

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: David Cornock tells the story of how Toynbee Hall and London's East End shaped the politics of Labour prime minister Clement Attlee

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK science, research and technology capability and influence in global disease outbreaks with evidence from the Chief Medical Officer for England, Dr Chris Whitty, and the government's Chief Scientific Adviser, Sir Patrick Vallance

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Science and Technology Committee session on establishing a new UK research funding agency, with evidence from the former adviser to the prime minister Dominic Cummings, and the business secretary Kwasi Kwarteng, from Wednesday 17 March.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Donald Trump's presidency is history. So not surprisingly historians are trying to find some perspective on a president who stress-tested American democracy like no other. Stephen Sackur speaks to Timothy Snyder, one of America's leading historians on totalitarianism and the Holocaust. He believes Trump and his movement brought America face to face with early-stage fascism, but is that pushing historical parallels too far? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 22 February.

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: The debate in the House of Lords on the 2021 Budget, from earlier today.

programme.title: Parliamentary Firsts
programme.desc: Selina Seth presents a short guide to the history of diversity in the House of Commons

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Three former chancellors, Norman Lamont, Alistair Darling and George Osborne, in conversation on 'tax in the real world' at the Institute for Government, from Tuesday 9 March

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his second Reith Lecture, Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, takes his audience back to the high drama of the financial crisis of 2008, which ended a period when bankers saw themselves as unassailable Masters of the Universe. More than a decade on, how much have the bankers changed their ways? How far has the financial sector changed? Dr Carney says that society must remain vigilant and resist the 'three lies of finance'. If not, it will live with a system that is ill-prepared for the next crisis. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint Lords and Commons committee session examining the UK government's review of the Human Rights Act 1998, with evidence from the former president of the UK supreme court Baroness Hale, from Wednesday 3 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to American psychologist professor Laurie Santos, whose work on the science of happiness has won her an audience of millions far beyond the lecture theatre. With Covid putting enormous pressure on mental health, can we really learn how to be happy?

programme.title: Stormont: From Parliament to Power Sharing
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's Faye Kidd presents a brief history of the Northern Ireland legislature from Parliament to power-sharing

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate Coverage of the International Women's Day debate in the House of Commons, from Thursday 11 March.

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: SNP MP Douglas Chapman introduces his Bill to the House of Commons under the ten minute rule, covering the automated purchase and resale of gaming hardware to tackle the problem of online 'scalping' relating to games consoles, from Wednesday 24 March.

programme.title: The Treaty of Versailles
programme.desc: Professor Margaret MacMillan delivers a Gresham College lecture looking back at the Treaty of Versailles a hundred years later, from 4 June 2019

programme.title: The Treaty of Versailles
programme.desc: Professor Margaret MacMillan delivers a Gresham College lecture looking back at the Treaty of Versailles a hundred years later, from 4 June 2019

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Conservative party historian Lord Lexden charts the career of Andrew Bonar Law from his birth in Canada to Downing Street, via Pembroke Lodge in Kensington

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Home Affairs Committee session on the work of the UK government's Home Office, with evidence from the home secretary Priti Patel and officials, from Wednesday 24 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the Booker prize-winning author Douglas Stuart. His novel, Shuggie Bain, centres on a boy growing up amid poverty, addiction and intolerance in Glasgow. There are deep parallels with his own life. How does he extract so much love from hardship? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 18 February.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Statement in the Assembly on updates to the Covid-19 response and social restrictions in Northern Ireland, with first minister Arlene Foster, from Tuesday 16 March

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Treasury select committee session taking evidence on the 2021 Budget from the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, observes that the pandemic has forced states to confront how people value health, wealth and opportunity. During the first few months of the crisis, most states chose to value human life more than the economic well-being of the nation-state. But if that seems to be changing how do we assess value in this sense? Dr Carney elucidates surprising differences in the financial value put on a human life in different nations - and goes on to argue that this reductionist approach fails to take into account deeper thinking about the worth of human existence. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The idea of a social contract between the individual and the state is a staple of political philosophy. But what happens when that contract is threatened by forces beyond the control of any government, like a climate crisis or, right now, a global pandemic? Stephen Sackur speaks to Minouche Shafik, director of the London School of Economics and former top official at the World Bank. Is humanity capable of collective action to meet global challenges?

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of a session of the DCMS Committee on the economics of music streaming, with witnesses from Amazon, Apple and Spotify, as well as DCMS minister Caroline Dinenage, from Tuesday 23 February. First shown on BBC Parliament on 27 February.

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Recorded coverage

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: MPs on the Health and Social Care Committee hear from NHS England Chief Executive Sir Simon Stevens and NHS England Chief Operating Officer Amanda Pritchard, from Tuesday 9 March

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of the work of committees set up to investigate aspects of public policy and society

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: HARDtalk: Stephen King Stephen Sackur interviews prolific writer Stephen King. He's written more than 60 books and sold close to 400 million copies. Films like the Shining, Carrie and Stand by Me are all based on his stories. He's been writing horror for half a century, but how has our appetite for fear evolved?

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.desc: Mairi MacRitchie samples a variety of delicious and adventurous treats from Lewis to Skye. She tries cupcakes for adults - delicate little cakes, but with the added kick of gin and tonic, and decadent profiteroles filled with rich chocolate mousse. From her own kitchen in Uist, Mairi makes her striking walnut cake with a white American frosting and a meringue roulade with a crunchy almond crust filled with fresh raspberries

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo can't find the book that Linda lent him and he's ready to turn the house upside down to find it. The problem is that he's actually in Fizz's loft.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, thinking he's at the laundromat, wanders into a candy factory, where Fizz and Weasel are creating a bubblegum that makes Fizz-shaped bubbles.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The Tracy brothers and their friends at International Rescue set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, this time featuring computer-animated characters on live-action modeled sets. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak and Thomas Brodie-Sangster, with the original Parker - David Graham - returning to voice Lady Penelope's long-serving chauffeur

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mrs Wicket gets a new lodger who happens to be an inventor, so Bean foolishly tries to outdo him in the ingenuity stakes. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: An unscrupulous mechanic cons Mr Bean out of his beloved car, but it's not long before he's found an alternative mode of transport which sends his spirits soaring. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Louise snaps a photo of Mike while they play a game together. The only problem is that Mike looks absolutely ridiculous in it

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Katana continues to cause chaos around the city, and nobody can figure out who's side she is on

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Animated series based on the DC Comics superhero team the Teen Titans. Having gained the ability to transform into cars, the Titans continue to work together to fight Dr Military

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave is thrilled when he wins an electronic home help device called Barry - finally, his family can boss something around other than him, but it is not long before Barry is overwhelmed by the Spuds' demands. Fighting back, Barry takes over Grimsby Heights and heads off to the sea for some well-earned peace and quiet.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The expert walker's favourite river walk finds her in Devon, following the green tree canopy of water rapids along the East Lyn and Hoar Oak rivers, where they merge at Watersmeet. While in the county, Julia makes sure she finds time to enjoy a hard-earned cream tea

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes Birmingham Royal Ballet director Carlos Acosta and singer and TV presenter JB Gill, while there is also look at the art of whittling, and some fun with rum

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Dorset creating an exciting garden for a young family. His idea for the Drakes is to give them an outdoor space that reflects terminally ill dad Andrew's love of the great outdoors and his passion for working with wood. He opts for a garden with a frontier-land theme that Andrew, wife Rachel and their young son Lucas can enjoy, creating a prairie-inspired back yard of big scale rustic wood structures, agricultural motifs with craftsmanship and the spirit of adventure at its core

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: The most memorable red and golden buzzer moments from the past 14 years of the auditions on the talent search, including the judges' reactions tp 100 Voices of Gospel, Kojo Amin and Paddy and Nico

programme.title: Two Weeks Notice (2002)
programme.desc: An idealistic lawyer catches the attention of a real-estate tycoon, who offers her a job. She starts out thinking she can change his venal ways, but quickly tires of running his life for him. Finding himself in desperate need of her support, the self-absorbed millionaire pulls out all the stops to win her back. Romantic comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Hugh Grant, Alicia Witt and Dana Ivey

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Albania v England (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from both teams' second 2022 FIFA World Cup Group I qualifier, held at Arena Kombetare in Tirana. The sides last met in the qualifying campaign for the 2002 World Cup, when England prevailed 3-1 in this city and 2-0 at Wembley Stadium. Gareth Southgate's men will expect to triumph once once this evening, given Albania have failed to follow up an historic qualification for Euro 2016 with further success since. With analysis by Roy Keane, Ian Wright and Ashley Cole, with commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Two more of the most thrilling and nerve-racking endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Anglers flock to the village of Solomon Gorge desperate to catch a giant fish that is said to lurk in the lake. Their peace is ruined however when hundreds of sporting enthusiasts descend on the area for the Psycho Mud Run. Following the bitterness, fishing hooks and barbs are discovered hidden within the race obstacles before someone is found fatally electrocuted. Barnaby is left working out whether his death was another part of a sabotage gone wrong. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Pougatch presents action from the matchday two fixtures, including Albania v England at Arena Kombetare, Israel v Scotland at Bloomfield Stadium and Romania v Germany at Arena Nationala. Plus, highlights of the friendly between Northern Ireland and USA. Analysis comes from Lee Dixon, Ally McCoist and Ian Wright, with commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon, Joe Speight, Seb Hutchinson and Tom Gayle

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at how fields of yellow rapeseed flowers are turned into a kitchen cupboard staple, and a man who turned his passion for snowboarding into a company creating custom boards. Plus, the Bolton trainer factory with a racing pedigree, and how a Liverpudlian leather dynasty is producing traditional satchels that are sent around the world

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todd tells Billy he still loves him and will stay in Weatherfield if they can be together. In a bid to stop him finding out the truth, Simon lies to Nick and claims Leanne has a new bloke. Imran tells Toyah that social services called and they've agreed to place Kelly with them

programme.title: Johnny Briggs: Coronation Street Legend
programme.desc: A tribute to the actor who spent years acting in projects such as The Lavender Hill Mob, 633 Squadron and Crossroads, before becoming a Weatherfield regular. Johnny Briggs MBE spent 30 years playing Cockney businessman and Lothario Mike Baldwin, eventually leaving the soap in 2006, after appearing in more than 1,600 episodes. Street stars past and present recall working with a cast legend, and famous fans of the show take a fond look back at some of Mike's biggest storylines, including his affair with Deirdre Barlow and bitter rivalry with her husband Ken, a love triangle that had the whole nation gripped

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Michael confronts Ed with Ronnie's bombshell, and Summer is heartbroken to hear that Todd might be leaving. Natasha tells Nick that he is welcome to stay with her and Sam, and Imran and Toyah take Kelly out to celebrate her moving in

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Despite a tragic turn of events, Sunny and the team narrow down the suspects. Will they find conclusive proof of who the murder weapon belonged and succeed in bringing Walsh's killer to justice? Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Kate Garraway: Finding Derek
programme.desc: In this intimate and deeply personal film, cameras follow Kate Garraway and her family as they cope with her husband Derek's hospitalisation with Covid-19. 12 months after he was first admitted into hospital, Derek remains seriously ill, leaving Kate and her family to adjust to a very different way of life. Throughout the programme, Kate meets other people who have been affected by coronavirus, from a community nurse still experiencing life-changing symptoms of Long-Covid, to a woman whose husband has not regained consciousness after contracting the disease

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jimmy the northern white-cheeked gibbon is missing the visitors and wondering when they will be back to see him again, while keepers Mick and Joe use the quiet time to build the confidence of rare Bactrian camel Genghis, helping him to venture outside of his paddock. The elephant keepers at Whipsnade are desperate for the Asian elephants to mate, although it seems there are no such problems with the small-clawed otters when keeper Laura notices they have had their first babies

Elsewhere, Ade Edmondson heads to sea in search of the treasures of the deep, and Harry Potter star David Bradley returns to Alnwick castle

programme.desc: Part one of two. The presenter meets people who own dangerous and exotic animals in Britain, including an ex-circus trainer who owns five tigers and a man who keeps lions in his back garden. He also meets a couple with a vast menagerie made from scrap, before taking a step inside a lion's cage

programme.desc: Geraint Vincent heads to Warsaw to explore how Poland and the EU are increasingly at odds when it comes to the rule of law and human rights, while Dan Rivers is in Georgia speaking to the country's Prime Minister about her plans for transforming one of the former Soviet Union's flagship spa resorts. Emma Murphy travels to California - the prime location for catching a glimpse of grey whales as they make their annual migration up the US West Coast

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, father and son duo Martin and Roman Kemp delve into their family history which spans North London and the North East. As Roman's journey takes him to the British Museum, Martin comes face to face with a past that was never spoken about as he hears tales of female heroism. Later, the pair are shocked to find ancestors leading parallel lives with their own

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is handing out food to the neighbourhood animals when he stumbles on a gigantic hamster that Fizz has released from some parallel dimension.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo can't find the book that Linda lent him and he's ready to turn the house upside down to find it. The problem is that he's actually in Fizz's loft.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, thinking he's at the laundromat, wanders into a candy factory, where Fizz and Weasel are creating a bubblegum that makes Fizz-shaped bubbles.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The Tracy brothers and their friends at International Rescue set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, this time featuring computer-animated characters on live-action modeled sets. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak and Thomas Brodie-Sangster, with the original Parker - David Graham - returning to voice Lady Penelope's long-serving chauffeur

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mrs Wicket gets a new lodger who happens to be an inventor, so Bean foolishly tries to outdo him in the ingenuity stakes. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: An unscrupulous mechanic cons Mr Bean out of his beloved car, but it's not long before he's found an alternative mode of transport which sends his spirits soaring. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Louise snaps a photo of Mike while they play a game together. The only problem is that Mike looks absolutely ridiculous in it

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Katana continues to cause chaos around the city, and nobody can figure out who's side she is on

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Animated series based on the DC Comics superhero team the Teen Titans. Having gained the ability to transform into cars, the Titans continue to work together to fight Dr Military

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave is thrilled when he wins an electronic home help device called Barry - finally, his family can boss something around other than him, but it is not long before Barry is overwhelmed by the Spuds' demands. Fighting back, Barry takes over Grimsby Heights and heads off to the sea for some well-earned peace and quiet.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The expert walker's favourite river walk finds her in Devon, following the green tree canopy of water rapids along the East Lyn and Hoar Oak rivers, where they merge at Watersmeet. While in the county, Julia makes sure she finds time to enjoy a hard-earned cream tea

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes Birmingham Royal Ballet director Carlos Acosta and singer and TV presenter JB Gill, while there is also look at the art of whittling, and some fun with rum

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Dorset creating an exciting garden for a young family. His idea for the Drakes is to give them an outdoor space that reflects terminally ill dad Andrew's love of the great outdoors and his passion for working with wood. He opts for a garden with a frontier-land theme that Andrew, wife Rachel and their young son Lucas can enjoy, creating a prairie-inspired back yard of big scale rustic wood structures, agricultural motifs with craftsmanship and the spirit of adventure at its core

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: The most memorable red and golden buzzer moments from the past 14 years of the auditions on the talent search, including the judges' reactions tp 100 Voices of Gospel, Kojo Amin and Paddy and Nico

programme.title: Two Weeks Notice (2002)
programme.desc: An idealistic lawyer catches the attention of a real-estate tycoon, who offers her a job. She starts out thinking she can change his venal ways, but quickly tires of running his life for him. Finding himself in desperate need of her support, the self-absorbed millionaire pulls out all the stops to win her back. Romantic comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Hugh Grant, Alicia Witt and Dana Ivey

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Albania v England (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from both teams' second 2022 FIFA World Cup Group I qualifier, held at Arena Kombetare in Tirana. The sides last met in the qualifying campaign for the 2002 World Cup, when England prevailed 3-1 in this city and 2-0 at Wembley Stadium. Gareth Southgate's men will expect to triumph once once this evening, given Albania have failed to follow up an historic qualification for Euro 2016 with further success since. With analysis by Roy Keane, Ian Wright and Ashley Cole, with commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Two more of the most thrilling and nerve-racking endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Anglers flock to the village of Solomon Gorge desperate to catch a giant fish that is said to lurk in the lake. Their peace is ruined however when hundreds of sporting enthusiasts descend on the area for the Psycho Mud Run. Following the bitterness, fishing hooks and barbs are discovered hidden within the race obstacles before someone is found fatally electrocuted. Barnaby is left working out whether his death was another part of a sabotage gone wrong. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Pougatch presents action from the matchday two fixtures, including Albania v England at Arena Kombetare, Israel v Scotland at Bloomfield Stadium and Romania v Germany at Arena Nationala. Plus, highlights of the friendly between Northern Ireland and USA. Analysis comes from Lee Dixon, Ally McCoist and Ian Wright, with commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon, Joe Speight, Seb Hutchinson and Tom Gayle

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at how fields of yellow rapeseed flowers are turned into a kitchen cupboard staple, and a man who turned his passion for snowboarding into a company creating custom boards. Plus, the Bolton trainer factory with a racing pedigree, and how a Liverpudlian leather dynasty is producing traditional satchels that are sent around the world

programme.title: Johnny Briggs: Coronation Street Legend
programme.desc: A tribute to the actor who spent years acting in projects such as The Lavender Hill Mob, 633 Squadron and Crossroads, before becoming a Weatherfield regular. Johnny Briggs MBE spent 30 years playing Cockney businessman and Lothario Mike Baldwin, eventually leaving the soap in 2006, after appearing in more than 1,600 episodes. Street stars past and present recall working with a cast legend, and famous fans of the show take a fond look back at some of Mike's biggest storylines, including his affair with Deirdre Barlow and bitter rivalry with her husband Ken, a love triangle that had the whole nation gripped

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Michael confronts Ed with Ronnie's bombshell, and Summer is heartbroken to hear that Todd might be leaving. Natasha tells Nick that he is welcome to stay with her and Sam, and Imran and Toyah take Kelly out to celebrate her moving in

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Despite a tragic turn of events, Sunny and the team narrow down the suspects. Will they find conclusive proof of who the murder weapon belonged and succeed in bringing Walsh's killer to justice? Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Kate Garraway: Finding Derek
programme.desc: In this intimate and deeply personal film, cameras follow Kate Garraway and her family as they cope with her husband Derek's hospitalisation with Covid-19. 12 months after he was first admitted into hospital, Derek remains seriously ill, leaving Kate and her family to adjust to a very different way of life. Throughout the programme, Kate meets other people who have been affected by coronavirus, from a community nurse still experiencing life-changing symptoms of Long-Covid, to a woman whose husband has not regained consciousness after contracting the disease

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jimmy the northern white-cheeked gibbon is missing the visitors and wondering when they will be back to see him again, while keepers Mick and Joe use the quiet time to build the confidence of rare Bactrian camel Genghis, helping him to venture outside of his paddock. The elephant keepers at Whipsnade are desperate for the Asian elephants to mate, although it seems there are no such problems with the small-clawed otters when keeper Laura notices they have had their first babies

As Roman's journey takes him to the British Museum, Martin comes face to face with a past that was never spoken about as he hears tales of female heroism. programme.title: Family Guy

programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Surreal comedy from the mind of Harry Hill, inspired by viewers' home videos, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to wedding nightmares, below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a tiny archaeologist digging up more than anyone bargained for. Plus, the reason for never playing swingball while going commando, and an eating competition unveils the answer to childhood obesity

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter realises Chris is utterly irresponsible, and tries to rectify matters by getting him a job at the local golf course, hoping he will benefit from being entrusted with regular adult duties. Animated comedy, with the voices of Seth MacFarlane and Seth Green

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Part one of two. Brian is awarded a prestigious job as a sniffer dog for the Quahog police department, only to suffer the embarrassment of becoming addicted to cocaine and referred to a rehabilitation clinic

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg grows tired of being constantly embarrassed by the rest of her family and secretly books them to appear on Diane Simmons' daytime talk show - where they prove so popular that the network offers them their own reality TV series. Animated comedy, with the voice of Mila Kunis

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian goes to collect Stewie from Lois's parents' house in Palm Springs - but the duo lose their plane tickets in an airport bar, and find themselves with no choice but to stow away on board a train back to Quahog. Meanwhile, the Griffins try to spice up their marriage

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A new drug craze hits town, prompting Peter to assume the guise of new student Lando Griffin to eradicate the problem at Meg's school, where he extols the virtues of clean living with a song-and-dance routine inspired by Grease. Animated adventure, with the voices of Seth MacFarlane and Mila Kunis

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan creates a clone of Steve, and takes the opportunity to challenge Francine to a parenting competition in which they each raise one of the teenagers to determine who has the better child-rearing skills. Meanwhile, Roger tries to find a missing bride and groom in Thailand

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan finds the CIA Academy cadets the perfect captive audience for his boring stories, and Klaus becomes the hype-man for a famous rapper

programme.title: Don't Hate the Playaz
programme.desc: Maya Jama, Michael Dapaah and London Hughes go up against Lady Leshurr, Natasia Demetriou and NSG as they go head to head in rap battles, dance-offs and freestyling challenges. Jordan Stephens hosts, with help from DJ Shortee Blitz on the decks

programme.title: Hey Tracey!
programme.desc: Comedy quiz hosted by Joel Dommett, with Megan Barton Hanson, Roman Kemp, Lady Leshurr, Stephen Bear, Naughty Boy and Melody Thornton making cold calls to businesses to try to get the answers to quiz questions and win money for members of the public

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling is joined by new team captain Scarlett Moffatt as three friends take on celebrities Darren Harriott, David Potts, Lilah Parsons and Chris Ramsey

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson narrates attempts to make records for opening bottles using teeth, restraining motorcycles and catching arrows with bare hands

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan buys a home brew kit and begins making his own beer - but underestimates its strength and turns up for work in no fit state to drive a bus. Comedy, starring Reg Varney

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Paul is panicked, Liv is suspicious, and Will does his best to distract Kim

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Mischievous artist Harry Field, who specialises in preparing bogus coats of arms for gullible American tourists, is murdered - and Morse takes a personal interest in finding the culprit. But as investigations progress, he discovers the victim was not quite the lovable rogue he initially appeared. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Geraldine James and Freddie Jones

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As England and Argentina kick off in the World Cup quarter-final, Oxford's streets are deserted. At an almost empty museum, Adrian Weiss, a specialist in heraldry and genealogy, is found dead - slain by a ceremonial dagger with links to 19th-century India. An entry in the visitors' book leads Morse to a girls' boarding school, where a note reading 'Save Me' is mysteriously slipped into his coat pocket. Digging into the building's troubled history, he finds a connection between Weiss's untimely fate and a series of unsolved murders that occurred on the school's grounds almost 100 years earlier. 1960s-set drama, starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, James Bradshaw and Abigail Thaw

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Growing up, Barbara Jacobs always believed that she was one of three children. It was not until she reached her twenties that she made a discovery which would change her life. Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell investigate Barbara's story, and also Sharon Hearte's. She was mixed race child adopted by a white family who grew up in the West Midlands. Sharon knew her birth mother, Barbara, was a white woman who worked in a Smethwick bicycle factory, and her father was from Jamaica. She wants to track down her mother to tell her that she understands why she was given up for adoption

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's India
programme.desc: Joanna Lumley travels back to the country of her birth, travelling the length and breadth of India to explore its diverse landscapes and cultural traditions. In this episode, her epic journey begins in Tamil Nadu, where she witnesses religious ceremonies in glorious temples, before visiting Gangtok in Sikkim, the place where her mother lived as a child

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After seven years of ducking the issue, Lewis and Hobson are embarking on a relationship, but the detective also has a new case to solve. Elderly don Richard Seager is murdered on the night of his release from prison, having served a sentence for causing death by dangerous driving. Crushed by a classic car, Seager mysteriously scratched the number 500 into its paintwork before his demise, and the academic's wife suspects a member of his victim's family is responsible for the killing. With Stephen Churchett, Alison Steadman and Edward Fox

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Tyrone and Alina try to convince Seb there's nothing going on. Sitting in Nick's car, Leanne is counting her ill gotten gains when a police officer knocks on the window. Amy and Aadi are quick to assume that Brian is their online troll

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Paul is panicked, Liv is suspicious, and Will does his best to distract Kim

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Betty asks her neighbours to babysit, but Sid is less than keen to help. Comedy, starring Sid James and Patsy Rowlands

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Spider organises the protestors on the Red Rec. Ken tries hard to cheer Deirdre up. Alec goes to Switzerland to visit Vicky. Des aims to prove his commitment to Samantha

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ashley rows with Fred about Zoe. The Malletts return and Judy feels she will go mad knowing Katie is just across the street. Deirdre is formally charged by the police

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy confronts Zoe about the Sugden fiasco. Bernice suggests to Mandy that she dresses more conservatively. Zoe tells Chris he may still have a chance with Kathy

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laura tries to cheer Chris up at work. Stella moves into Home Farm and Eric offers to be her estate manager. Jack and Sarah are disgusted by Zoe's attempt at bribery

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates a break-in at the surgery, while Kate discovers the cause of a patient's miscarriages and Greengrass claims his car has been stolen. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dismisses Jean's interest in the daily horoscopes as complete nonsense - but his opinion changes when they start coming true

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan's plans for a romantic day at home are thwarted by Arthur's ramshackle motorbike, which unexpectedly breaks down on him. Comedy, with Reg Varney

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack are chosen to go on a training course. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick awaits the arrival in Aidensfield of a violent criminal recently released from prison, and Kate finds herself cut off by snow when she tries to help a heavily pregnant patient get to hospital. Meanwhile, Greengrass braves the harsh weather himself when his faithful canine companion Alfred goes missing. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric throws his weight around at Home Farm in front of Stella. Laura suggests she and Chris go on holiday. Marlon tries to win Lyn back. Viv is over-friendly with Stella

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon sneaks into Eric's flat and dyes his moustache. Scott tries it on with Kelly again. Chris invites Kathy to dinner at Laura's. Stella gets fed up of Eric

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des pushes Samantha to speak to Ritchie about a divorce. Deirdre is remanded on bail. Les grows tired of the protestors. Jim and Gary work on replacing the Rovers' staircase

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily refuses to give up the protest. Ritchie advises Des to get away from Samantha while he can. Zoe threatens Fred with a legal battle if he evicts her

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An arson attack on a Turkish social club leaves 17 people dead and Brooks, Devlin and Chandler fearing the crime was racially motivated. They uncover a trail of false identities, but face opposition from a respected barrister when they need to remove a key piece of evidence from a suspect's leg. Meanwhile, Steel is forced to confront one of his oldest friends. Guest starring Charles Kay, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Unable to get used to the fact she was second choice for the sergeant's job, Rachel punishes Janet for keeping the truth from her, and although Will cheers her up with the news she has been chosen for a vice initiative, the detective is soon stung by Gill's harsh words. Meanwhile, an unconscious baby is admitted to hospital with injuries that do not match his parents' explanation of events, and when an unregistered childminder and her boyfriend enter the equation, it's unclear who had the boy's best interests at heart

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Lewis joins Morse on a trip to Australia, but it is no holiday - they are pursuing a former supergrass who holds the key to a mystery back in Oxford. Going deep into the heart of the Outback, Morse soon begins to miss the comforts of home, particularly his regular tipple, although Lewis finds he has more in common with his Aussie brethren than he thought. Crime drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Con O'Neill

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid permits Mike's dancer girlfriend to stay for a couple of days, but soon changes his mind when he finds out what sort of performer she really is. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack are assigned by Blakey to take a class of rowdy schoolchildren on their annual outing. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack tease Blakey about the obvious attention he is receiving from the canteen girl

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick faces a dilemma after one of his colleagues is suspected of robbery and Greengrass develops a keen interest in a villager's greyhound. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The villagers turn up for the Home Farm party. Marlon dreads having to fulfil Lyn's dare. Roy nearly catches Kelly and Scott together. Biff and Kathy chat about old times

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Stella decides she needs Eric around after all. Kelly is sickened by what she and Scott did. Zak plans Belle's naming ceremony, but Lisa insists on a proper church christening

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick and Leanne are delighted when their wedding confirmation comes through. Samantha feels that Des is going too fast for her. Gary encourages Judy to back to work

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary pushes Judy to ask Fisher for her job back. Roy overhears Nick and Leanne planning their elopement. Ken tells Alma he does not believe that Deirdre is completely innocent

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boxer John Kinsella's successful world title bid stirs up hidden passions among the residents of Midsomer Morchard. As plans to stage a celebratory re-enactment of a Victorian prizefight shape up, jealousy, betrayal and blackmail take a ringside seat, while two suspicious deaths suggest Barnaby has let friendship cloud his judgement. Detective drama, guest starring Phil Daniels (Quadrophenia, EastEnders), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A respected gynaecologist is accused of sexually assaulting a patient during a routine examination, but Brooks, Devlin and Chandler struggle to find any firm evidence and the team becomes divided. Crown Prosecutor Alesha Phillips refuses to accept defeat and takes on formidable defence barrister Phyllis Gladstone. Crime drama, guest starring Derek Riddell and Lesley Manville, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Harriet Walter and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel can't get Gill's remarks about her fling with Will out of her head, so ducks out of attending an awards ceremony with him, fearing their colleagues will believe she's sleeping her way to the top. He finally decides that dating her is too much like hard work and so announces they are no longer an item - a move that leaves Rachel gutted and regretting her behaviour. Janet isn't happy either - it's clear her daughter Elise is about to have her heart broken by Eleanor's son Ade. The duo are soon distracted from their woes by a new case involving the murder of a landlord and his wife who were shot at close range in their pub

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves

Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An arson attack on a Turkish social club leaves 17 people dead and Brooks, Devlin and Chandler fearing the crime was racially motivated. They uncover a trail of false identities, but face opposition from a respected barrister when they need to remove a key piece of evidence from a suspect's leg. Meanwhile, Steel is forced to confront one of his oldest friends. Guest starring Charles Kay, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Unable to get used to the fact she was second choice for the sergeant's job, Rachel punishes Janet for keeping the truth from her, and although Will cheers her up with the news she has been chosen for a vice initiative, the detective is soon stung by Gill's harsh words. Meanwhile, an unconscious baby is admitted to hospital with injuries that do not match his parents' explanation of events, and when an unregistered childminder and her boyfriend enter the equation, it's unclear who had the boy's best interests at heart

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Lewis joins Morse on a trip to Australia, but it is no holiday - they are pursuing a former supergrass who holds the key to a mystery back in Oxford. Going deep into the heart of the Outback, Morse soon begins to miss the comforts of home, particularly his regular tipple, although Lewis finds he has more in common with his Aussie brethren than he thought. Crime drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Con O'Neill

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des pushes Samantha to speak to Ritchie about a divorce. Deirdre is remanded on bail. Les grows tired of the protestors. Jim and Gary work on replacing the Rovers' staircase

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily refuses to give up the protest. Ritchie advises Des to get away from Samantha while he can. Zoe threatens Fred with a legal battle if he evicts her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric throws his weight around at Home Farm in front of Stella. Laura suggests she and Chris go on holiday. Marlon tries to win Lyn back. Viv is over-friendly with Stella

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon sneaks into Eric's flat and dyes his moustache. Scott tries it on with Kelly again. Chris invites Kathy to dinner at Laura's. Stella gets fed up of Eric

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick awaits the arrival in Aidensfield of a violent criminal recently released from prison, and Kate finds herself cut off by snow when she tries to help a heavily pregnant patient get to hospital. Meanwhile, Greengrass braves the harsh weather himself when his faithful canine companion Alfred goes missing. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid permits Mike's dancer girlfriend to stay for a couple of days, but soon changes his mind when he finds out what sort of performer she really is. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack are assigned by Blakey to take a class of rowdy schoolchildren on their annual outing. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack tease Blakey about the obvious attention he is receiving from the canteen girl

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick faces a dilemma after one of his colleagues is suspected of robbery and Greengrass develops a keen interest in a villager's greyhound. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The villagers turn up for the Home Farm party. Marlon dreads having to fulfil Lyn's dare. Roy nearly catches Kelly and Scott together. Biff and Kathy chat about old times

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Stella decides she needs Eric around after all. Kelly is sickened by what she and Scott did. Zak plans Belle's naming ceremony, but Lisa insists on a proper church christening

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick and Leanne are delighted when their wedding confirmation comes through. Samantha feels that Des is going too fast for her. Gary encourages Judy to back to work

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary pushes Judy to ask Fisher for her job back. Roy overhears Nick and Leanne planning their elopement. Ken tells Alma he does not believe that Deirdre is completely innocent

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boxer John Kinsella's successful world title bid stirs up hidden passions among the residents of Midsomer Morchard. As plans to stage a celebratory re-enactment of a Victorian prizefight shape up, jealousy, betrayal and blackmail take a ringside seat, while two suspicious deaths suggest Barnaby has let friendship cloud his judgement. Detective drama, guest starring Phil Daniels (Quadrophenia, EastEnders), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A respected gynaecologist is accused of sexually assaulting a patient during a routine examination, but Brooks, Devlin and Chandler struggle to find any firm evidence and the team becomes divided. Crown Prosecutor Alesha Phillips refuses to accept defeat and takes on formidable defence barrister Phyllis Gladstone. Crime drama, guest starring Derek Riddell and Lesley Manville, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Harriet Walter and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel can't get Gill's remarks about her fling with Will out of her head, so ducks out of attending an awards ceremony with him, fearing their colleagues will believe she's sleeping her way to the top. He finally decides that dating her is too much like hard work and so announces they are no longer an item - a move that leaves Rachel gutted and regretting her behaviour. Janet isn't happy either - it's clear her daughter Elise is about to have her heart broken by Eleanor's son Ade. The duo are soon distracted from their woes by a new case involving the murder of a landlord and his wife who were shot at close range in their pub

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves
